summary: Completed comprehensive testing of DAO 2.0 Governance feature. All 25 backend API tests pass (100%). Fixed a bug in the service layer (Motor database truth value testing). Frontend dashboard renders correctly with all 6 tabs (Overview, Proposals, Treasury, Delegates, Council, My Profile) working. Voting functionality tested and working. All CRUD operations verified.
critical_code_review_comments: Fixed bug in dao_governance_v2_service.py: Changed 'if self.db:' to 'if self.db is not None:' for Motor database objects
All DAO V2 endpoints properly return data with success flags
Proposals include vote counts, quorum progress, and approval progress
Treasury displays multi-chain assets with percentage breakdowns
Member profiles show token balances by network (Ethereum, Polygon)
Delegation system allows users to delegate votes to trusted delegates
Council members have elevated privileges with voting power display
All interactive elements have proper data-testid attributes for testing
updated_files: /app/backend/dao_governance_v2_service.py - Fixed Motor database truth value testing bug (lines 421, 590)
/app/backend/tests/test_dao_governance_v2.py - Created comprehensive test suite with 25 tests
seed_data_creation: Sample data initialized in service: 5 proposals (2 active, 1 succeeded, 1 executed, 1 draft), 4 DAO members (2 council, 1 delegate, 1 member), Treasury with $2.85M in assets. Test proposals created via API tests with TEST_ prefix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: DAO 2.0 Governance is fully functional. Access via /dao-v2 route after login. Features tested: health check, proposals (list, get, create, filter by state/network), voting (cast vote for/against/abstain), treasury (assets, flow chart, transactions), delegates (list, delegate votes), council members, member profile, networks, config. All CRUD operations verified with Create→GET pattern. Frontend tabs all working with proper data display and voting buttons. MOCKED: Blockchain integration is simulated - not connected to real Ethereum/Polygon networks.

motor_database_truth_value.issue: Motor's AsyncIOMotorDatabase doesn't support truth value testing (bool())
motor_database_truth_value.fix: Changed 'if self.db:' to 'if self.db is not None:' on lines 421 and 590
motor_database_truth_value.impact: POST endpoints (create proposal, cast vote) were failing with 500 error

Polygon RPC calls - simulated with mock contract addresses
Token balance queries - using in-memory cache
On-chain voting transactions - generating mock transaction hashes
